title: WebSocket handler for terminal sessions with input/output management
purpose: Manages WebSocket connections for terminal sessions, handling input, output, resizing, and connection lifecycle events.
module_context: Exports four WebSocket event handlers: handleTerminalOpen, handleTerminalMessage, handleTerminalClose, and handleTerminalError. Defines types for client and server messages, and terminal WebSocket data structure.

steps.content: Serialize the error message into a JSON object.
Send the serialized error message to the WebSocket client.
IF sending fails, log a warning about the failure.
Extract the session ID from the WebSocket data.
IF the session ID is missing or invalid, send an error and close the connection.
ELSE attach the WebSocket to the terminal session.
IF attachment fails, send an error and close the connection.
Log successful connection to the terminal session.
Extract the session ID from the WebSocket data.
IF the session ID is missing, send an error and return.
Parse the incoming message as JSON.
IF parsing fails, send an error and return.
Validate and process the message based on its type.
IF the message type is input, write the data to the terminal session.
IF the message type is resize, update the terminal dimensions.
IF the message type is unknown, send an error and return.
IF processing fails, send an error to the client.
Extract the session ID from the WebSocket data.
IF the session ID is missing, return immediately.
Detach the WebSocket from the terminal session.
Log successful disconnection from the terminal session.
IF detaching fails, log the error.
Extract the session ID from the WebSocket data.
Log the WebSocket error with the session ID.
IF the session ID exists, detach the WebSocket from the terminal session.
IF detaching fails, log the error.
